sections.title: A Journey Through a Forest
sections.content_summary: Reena and Amit travel to their village near a forest for a harvest festival that coincides with their village becoming fully solar-powered. The narrative introduces the concept of solar energy powering a village and sets the context for exploring nature-based village life. The section establishes the dual celebration — a harvest festival and a clean-energy milestone — as the story's framing device.

sections.title: Welcome to the Village!
sections.content_summary: On entering the village, children see the Palash tree whose orange-red blooms give it the name 'flame of forest'; the textbook notes the local name Kesuda used in Gujarat. Village houses are built from clay, hay, and cow dung with wall paintings made using rice flour and water dye — traditional practices the grandmother demonstrates. The chapter also introduces natural dyes made from plant extracts (flowers, leaves, powdered coloured stones) and traditional grain-storage methods using earthen pots lined with neem leaves and bamboo baskets coated with cow dung to repel insects. The Jenu Kurubas tribe of Karnataka is highlighted for singing apology songs to bees when harvesting honey, illustrating Indigenous respect for nature.

tasks.task_text: Write names of five colourful flowers in your area. For example, Palash is called Kesuda in Gujarat.
Make the model of a house using materials from nature such as clay, wooden sticks, hay, grass, leaves, etc. Decorate it with an art form of your choice.

Let us prepare a natural dye. Collect some flowers, leaves, barks, and roots, such as beetroot, hibiscus, and marigold that have colouring properties. Boil any of the collected plant parts in approximately 1–2 litres of water for 30 minutes to an hour covering the container with a plate. Strain the liquid to remove the plant materials, leaving behind the natural dye. Now, try to paint a piece of cloth such as a light-coloured handkerchief or hand towel using the dye you have prepared.
Find out some traditional ways of preserving grains and vegetables at your place, and fill the following table. One example is given.
Discuss with the elders in your family and write the name of the plant, its parts, and its uses, in the table given below.
What things should be kept in a first-aid box?
Why do you think providing first-aid is important if someone gets injured?

sections.title: Sparkling Light, Happy Life
sections.content_summary: The village celebration begins with the solar-powered village lit up for the harvest festival; people wear traditional clothes and jewels, offer prayers to God and mother nature, and dance to dhol and nagada drums. The section explores India's tradition of nature worship through festivals such as Vat Purnima (Banyan tree), Hari Jiroti (fruit-bearing trees), and the Kaziranga Elephant Festival. Sacred Groves are introduced as small forest patches protected by local communities for worship and wildlife conservation. The section concludes with concrete ways to care for nature — reducing paper waste, avoiding plastic, planting trees, feeding birds, and organising clean-up drives.

tasks.task_text: Find out the traditional dances and songs of your region. Perform a traditional dance with the help of your classmates and teachers for the Annual Day of your school.
Imagine if there is no electricity in your area for two days. What changes will you see in your life?
Talk to your elders and find out the rituals in connection to animals and plants which are celebrated in your locality. A few examples are given below.
What are the things that we use from nature in our daily life?
What challenges arise when we overuse natural resources? For example, if we overuse wood from nature, our forests deplete. Water: ___ Soil: ___ Sea products: ___ Any other: ___
How can we protect the natural environment around us?
Design your herbal garden. Draw pictures of your favorite herbs in different parts of the garden and write down their names. Are there some herbs that you like less? Draw them too!

dual_strand.natural: Forests, plants, and natural materials as resources for human life
dual_strand.human_cultural: Traditional practices of conservation, craftsmanship, and nature worship in Indian communities
